_comment: stats_sources feed the numeric views (Now / Wind / Waves / Tides) via stats.json. tabs is the ordered list the display cycles through, 'type' drives how each renders: overview = glanceable Now screen; wind/waves = big number(s) + compass/trend PLUS an animated regional map built from many PNGs (the 'frames' block); tides = current state + hi/lo schedule; image = a single map. A 'frames' block is scraped live each run (frame filenames shift per model run), all frames downloaded, and the display animates them frame_ms apart. frame_take: 'first' = soonest frames (forecast), 'last' = most recent (nowcast).

tabs.label: Radar
tabs.note: Animated loop. KAKQ covers the Northern Neck. Capped by the ~30 min cron.

frames.note: NCCOS sea nettle probability, Rappahannock/York region (Box 6). The source publishes 8 daily frames = 6 past days + today + 1 forecast day; frame_take:last with max_frames:2 keeps only today + tomorrow (the model's 1-day forecast horizon), dropping the past days. Seasonal; expect it offline in winter.
